question_text: What is the moment of a force?

options.label: A
options.text: The size of the force applied to an object

options.label: B
options.text: The turning effect of a force around a pivot

options.label: C
options.text: The time it takes for a force to act

correct_answer: The turning effect of a force around a pivot
explanation: A moment is the turning effect that happens when a force acts at a distance from a pivot point. It's what makes things rotate or turn. The moment is calculated by multiplying the force by how far it acts from the pivot (moment = force × perpendicular distance from pivot).
